title: NOC 60031 – Accommodation Service Managers | GTR Immigration
description: NOC 60031 professionals are the strategic pilots of Canada’s multi-billion-dollar tourism industry. From luxury resorts in the Rockies to major city hotels, accommodation service managers ensure that visitors experience the very best of Canadian hospitality. This TEER 0 managerial occupation is highly valued by IRCC, making it a "prime" category for those looking to secure PR through targeted provincial draws and the Express Entry system.

classification.teer: Classification LevelDetailsBroad Occupation Category6 – Sales and service occupationsTEER Level0Major Group60 – Executive and senior management occupationsSub-Major Group600 – Management occupationsMinor Group6003 – Managers in food service and accommodationNOC Code60031

duties: Develop and implement policies for the operation of the department or establishment.
Prepare budgets and monitor revenues and expenses.
Participate in the development of pricing and promotional strategies.
Recruit and supervise staff, oversee training, and set work schedules.
Negotiate with clients for the use of facilities for conventions and functions.
employment_requirements: A university degree or college diploma in hotel management or a related discipline is usually required for large establishments.
Several years of experience within the accommodation industry are required and may substitute for formal education in smaller settings.
job_outlook: The outlook is Favourable to Very Good. Growth is driven by the expansion of the "bleisure" (business + leisure) travel market. Over 14,000 new positions are expected to be filled by 2031.
labour_market_demand: With Canada hosting more international conventions and events, the demand for hotel and resort managers is at an all-time high. Provinces like Alberta and Ontario are currently seeing a "Very Good" job outlook as the travel sector exceeds pre-pandemic performance levels.
Elite Branding: Opportunity to work with prestigious global hotel chains.
Career Trajectory: Rapid progression to General Manager or Regional Director roles.
Stable Market: Strong domestic and international tourism ensures long-term job security.
pathways: Express Entry (Federal Skilled Worker): Priority as a TEER 0 managerial role.
Ontario INP (OINP): Frequent draws for skilled workers in the service sector.
Alberta Opportunity Stream: A dedicated path for those currently working in the province.
